  <h2 id="lecture_heading" class="section-title dsp-flex-xs flex-align-items-center-xs" data-lecture-id="9061808" data-next-lecture-id="17070063" data-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/9061808" data-next-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/17070063" ,="" data-previous-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/46904991" data-previous-lecture-id="46904991">

    11. You Have A Social Disease (The Instagram Rules)
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=2 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=18053572, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-18053572">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><em>"I have a social disease."</em></p><p>That's the first line of Andy Warhol's 1979 photo book: 'Exposures'.</p><p>And in classic Warhol cultural forecasting... He was right again. We all have a social disease.</p><p><strong>But social media is not the enemy. The way we use social media... is.</strong></p><p>I predict that by 2021 the way we use social media will transform completely.</p><p>Remember... </p><p>There was a time that people used to smoke in bars, restaurants and even airplanes. And look at how now that's completely frowned upon and the world has amicably adjusted to it.</p><p>That's what's gonna happen to social media also.</p><p>People are already sick of it. </p><p>They just don't know HOW to stop scrolling. 'Cause that's where their friends are, etc...</p><p>But the goal is not to stop scrolling. The goal is not to 'disappear off the grid'.</p><p><em>You need to change YOURSELF.</em></p><p>Don't use social media to show your best 'side'. Use it to show your best 'message'. Stop posting at the "mass-user determined optimal times". Post at the frequency that empowers you. Use the algorithm in your favor by being, gasp... social. Post a photo and then spend the next three days commenting on it. Be the person at the party that brings "life" to it. Make no mistake, social media is a party. And marketing agencies are the pushers trying to sell us their trends and hacks for a new high... Don't buy into it. It's open bar. Get a little tipsy and dance the night away. But never be the last to leave. </p><p>You're not here to serve an algorithm. You're here to serve your audience. When you give your fans quality revolutionary goods, the algorithm is designed to reward you. Facebook and Instagram are trying to give their audience the best online experience. Why aren't you?</p><p>Produce things that are rare and valuable... and everyone wins.</p><p>Don’t post just to post. </p><p>Don’t post because someone else posted. </p><p>Don’t post because you have too many photos on your drive. </p><p>Don’t post because you're bored. </p><p>Don’t post to make someone jealous.</p><p>Don't post in knee-jerk reaction to haters.</p><p><strong>Post to glorify your journey. </strong></p><p>Stop trying to beat the algorithm. Instead, try to emulate the algorithm. Even better... Create your own algorithm. It’s called "posting what my true fans need to hear", not just what will make the masses double-tap.</p><p><em>When you focus on the algorithm you end up programming yourself to behave like everyone else.</em></p><p>When most people say 'social media' they're talking about Instagram. Some may check Facebook every day, but most people can't function without Instagram.</p><p>But people forget that Instagram is a service. Your pictures, videos and captions all reside on rented land. You're at the mercy of Instagram's whims, their algorithms, their politics and their punishments too. All you can do while you play by their rules is to use it to keep furthering your own values and principles. If you have none, then you'll just end up doing what everyone else is doing: wasting time.</p><p><em>And stop saying Instagram is free. Instagram is NOT free. Its cost to your mental health... is staggering.</em></p><p>I predict that the "perfect lives" we see on social will one day be a thing of the past. Imperfection is about to be on trend. Not the fake-ass "I don't look cute (but I actually do)" photos we're seeing, but an avalanche of authenticity that will be like Nirvana descending on the boy bands. You're gonna start seeing more and more of those IG model "breakdowns" and "posting what is real" rebellions. </p><p>The modern marketer will tell you that it's not about likes anymore. It's about engagement. </p><p>The new wave marketer will tell you that it's not about engagement, it's about retention. It's about surrounding yourself with people that want EVERY LAST DROP! </p><p>Make "content" for <em>those</em> people.</p><p><strong>If you're not tending to that kind of following it's because you don't have one. And if you don't have one it's because you have nothing to say. <em>You have no message.</em></strong></p><p>Find what makes you unique and double-down on it. Even if it isn't popular. That's called your NICHE.</p><p>Figure out your message and add it to your visual content. Just like having meaningful captions gives people value on an individual post... Having a message gives people more value on an individual profile.</p><p>I did a test. </p><p>I uploaded an 11 panel, text-only Instagram Story instead of the crazy videos that I usually do... It got more engagement yes... but most of all it had an 80% retention rate. The people that got to the end? Those are my people.</p><p>And guess where I found the words for that IG Story? </p><p>From an email that I had written... See how new wave marketing works?</p><p>I told one of my students to get rid of her IG. None of her people were on it. And besides, she hated it. But she kept it for appearances. But after she got rid of it she could proudly say "I'm not on IG either" and attract more of her like-minded following. IRL. </p><p>But of course most of us can't get rid of our IG. Not because we're addicted to it of course... (because we're not right? haha...) No, we can't get rid of it because <em>OUR AUDIENCE IS ADDICTED TO IT</em>. And if that's where they congregate to manifest their addiction, you'll have to meet them there to satisfy their needs. But give them something of value for when they land on your stuff. <em>Make art not content.</em></p><p>So tell me again... Why are you updating every day? </p><p>Oh, your marketing agency told you that you need to update at least once a day so that “more people can see and engage with your content”. And that "you have to keep updating so that you're top-of-mind with your customers?" </p><p>That's straight out of the IG playbook that they've bought into. </p><p>Most businesses should only be using Instagram as a store front and only be updating twice a week... just to make sure that anyone that lands there is getting the right message.</p><p>If you want to stay top-of-mind, then use DMs. Get to know your audience for REAL.</p><p>If you have a 1000 followers then only 100 of them will see your post anyways...</p><p><strong>Do you really think you moved the needle today by posting a picture and replying in the "comments" with a few heart emojis?</strong></p><p>If your relationship with your fans begins and ends on Instagram then you don't have an "Instagram plan"... You have an I.L.O. -- "Instagram Lost Opportunity". </p><p>These marketing companies give you the illusion that they're working... by adding content every day. But the sad truth is that you're still not growing and you're still not connecting.</p><p>Oh and another thing.</p><p><em>Look at your marketing agency’s Instagram. See how much it sucks? That's who you've entrusted your “precious real-estate” to.</em></p><p></p>
</div>
